editorial_data_card.label: SaaS Growth Insight
editorial_data_card.headline: Your Onboarding Costs 3× More Than Your Acquisition

stats.context: Avg cost to onboard one user

editorial_data_card.insight: Every manual onboarding step is revenue leaking out. Most SaaS teams spend 40%+ of their support budget on activation issues that should be self-serve.

transformation.headline: What Changed When We Stopped Guessing at Churn
after_items: CS reaches out 14 days before risk

steps.title: Usage Data Pull
steps.description: Export feature usage by plan tier. Find which features paid users actually use vs. which ones they're paying for but ignoring.

steps.title: Willingness-to-Pay Survey
steps.description: Ask 50 customers what they'd pay for each feature independently. The gaps between perceived and actual value reveal your mispricing.

steps.title: Competitor Plan Mapping
steps.description: Map every competitor's pricing page. Not to copy — to find what they bundle that you give away free.

steps.title: Revenue Impact Model
steps.description: Model three scenarios: price increase, new tier, feature unbundling. Pick the one that grows revenue without spiking churn.

split_comparison.headline: Why 90% of SaaS Companies Plateau at $1M ARR
bad_items: Hire 5 sales reps before PMF is proven
Build features customers asked for once
Spend 60% of budget on paid acquisition
Measure MRR but ignore activation rate
good_items: Obsess over time-to-value for new signups
Build the feature that makes users stay
Track activation rate as the #1 metric
Self-serve path that converts without sales

bold_text_metaphor.headline: Your Pricing Page Is the Most Ignored Growth Lever
bold_text_metaphor.caption: Opinion · SaaS Pricing
bold_text_metaphor.metaphor_html: 1 pricing changedoubled revenue without adding a single customer

quote_card.quote: The best SaaS teams don't sell harder. They activate faster — then barely need to sell at all.
quote_card.context: After 10 years building B2B products, the single highest-ROI investment isn't acquisition. It's reducing time-to-value.
